summary: Strong mid‑80s show with standout versions of I Know You Rider, Man Smart (Woman Smarter) and Little Red Rooster, captured on a solid soundboard recording despite occasional mix quirks.
review: The performance shines in the second set – Jerry’s lead work is blazing, Phil erupts on the China > Rider sequence and the band delivers a ferocious Little Red Rooster and an astonishing I Know You Rider. The SBD source is generally clear and easy on the ears, though Bobby’s guitar can be buried at times and a few songs (China Rider, Far From Me) feel a bit flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and note good soundboard quality; only minor criticisms about specific songs and occasional mix issues keep the rating just above a solid four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced SBD with pitch fixes; some listeners note Bobby’s guitar buried in parts and occasional roughness typical of an open‑air venue, but overall easy on the ears.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid but unremarkable (C+), while the second set ramps up with a surprising sequence of high‑energy jams and strong finishes, earning a B‑ rating from reviewers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Blazing lead guitar throughout; called the strongest player in the show, though his vocals slip on Far From Me.
band_member_comments.Phil: Turns heads with fiery bass work, especially during the China > Rider jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides solid rhythm and vocal support, but noted for comical croaking on Far From Me.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s solo in The Women is described as a bit biffed; also highlighted as taking lead vocals on some moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Great beam episode during the Drums > Space excursion praised.
